title: Mailbox message family typed payloads (10 Phase-1 families)
goal: Define the typed message-family payloads required by spec v02.186 Â§02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsection [ADD v02.173]: `delegate_work`, `blocker`, `review_request`, `decision_request`, `announce_back`, `micro_task_request`, `micro_task_feedback`, `micro_task_verification_needed`, `micro_task_escalation`, `micro_task_completion_report`. Each family gets a strongly typed body schema, validator helper, and serde round-trip test. Replaces the MT-176 placeholder MessageType enum with a real typed family hierarchy.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/families.rs with one struct per family (DelegateWorkBody, BlockerBody, ReviewRequestBody, DecisionRequestBody, AnnounceBackBody, MicroTaskRequestBody, MicroTaskFeedbackBody, MicroTaskVerificationNeededBody, MicroTaskEscalationBody, MicroTaskCompletionReportBody) and a `MessageFamily` enum tagged serde wrapper; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_families_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-176 message primitive scaffold lands the placeholder MessageType enum that this MT replaces.
implementation_notes: Per spec subsection [ADD v02.173] line 6146 (Phase 1 Role Mailbox message families): define each family as a separate Rust struct. Common fields belong to RoleMailboxMessage (MT-176); family-specific fields live on body structs. Sketch: `DelegateWorkBody { task_summary: String, target_role: RoleId, due_at_utc: Option, linked_wp: Option<String>, linked_mt: Option<String> }`; `BlockerBody { blocker_description: String, blocking_role: Option<RoleId>, severity: BlockerSeverity (Soft|Hard) }`; `ReviewRequestBody { review_target: ReviewTarget (WorkPacket|MicroTask|Patch|Other(String)), review_target_id: String, review_kind: ReviewKind (CodeReview|GovReview|SpecReview), evidence_pointers: Vec<EvidencePointer> }`; `DecisionRequestBody { question: String, options: Vec<DecisionOption>, decision_authority_role: RoleId, deadline_utc: Option }`; `AnnounceBackBody { sub_session_id: Option<Uuid v7>, summary: String, artifacts: Vec<ArtifactPointer>, completion_state: CompletionState }`; the 5 micro_task_* families embed `MicroTaskRef { wp_id, mt_id, iteration_n: u32 }` plus family-specific fields (e.g., MicroTaskEscalationBody adds `escalation_target: EscalationTier`, `reason: String`, `prior_attempts: Vec<PriorAttemptRef>`). The wrapper enum `MessageFamily` uses `#[serde(tag = "family", content = "body")]` so JSON round-trips cleanly. Per spec line 6147 the 5 micro_task_* families MUST embed a `micro_task_executor_contract_ref: MicroTaskExecutorContractRef` field that points to the bounded loop contract (populated downstream in X.2 â€” MT-184/MT-185 produce the executor surface; the type is forward-declared here as an opaque newtype). Tests: (a) serialize and deserialize each family body, expect round-trip equality; (b) decode a fixture corpus of pre-authored example payloads (committed under tests/fixtures/role_mailbox_families/) for each family; (c) reject a message with `family="micro_task_request"` but missing `micro_task_executor_contract_ref`.
risk_if_missed: Without typed family payloads, mailbox messages become untyped JSON blobs; validators cannot enforce per-family invariants; the spec's bounded micro_task_* family constraints (line 6147) silently degrade to 'keep trying' loops.
validator_focus: Every family round-trips serde without information loss; micro_task_* families enforce the executor_contract_ref invariant; unknown families serialize to a typed `Unknown` variant rather than panicking (forward-compat for downstream family additions).

minimum_controls: Forward-compat: unknown family decodes to `MessageFamily::Unknown { raw: serde_json::Value }` instead of error so future families do not break the repo.
Property-based test (use proptest) generates arbitrary bodies and asserts round-trip equality.
Negative test asserts micro_task_* families without contract_ref fail deserialization with a typed error.
